referenced_section_hint: section 11AF
description: This Prudential Standard is made under section 11AF of the Banking Act 1959.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Measurement of Capital — deductions from CET1, Tier 1, Total Capital
description: Assets required to be deducted from CET1, Tier 1 or Total Capital under APS 111 are excluded from the scope of APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Securitisation — securitisation exposures
description: Securitisation exposures subject to APS 120 are excluded from APS 112 scope, except for funding-only or synthetic securitisations where underlying exposures must be included.

referenced_section_hint: Covered Bonds — liabilities of covered bond SPV
description: Liabilities of a covered bond special purpose vehicle to an issuing ADI as specified in APS 121 are excluded from APS 112 scope.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Market Risk — items subject to market risk capital requirements
description: Items subject to APS 116 market risk capital requirements that do not have counterparty credit risk under APS 180 are excluded from APS 112 scope.

referenced_section_hint: Capital Adequacy: Counterparty Credit Risk
description: APS 180 is referenced for the definition of counterparty credit risk and for items excluded from APS 112 scope.

referenced_section_hint: Definitions
description: Terms defined in APS 001 Definitions appear in bold on first use in APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Credit Risk Management — non-performing exposure definition
description: Defaulted exposure is defined by reference to the non-performing exposure definition in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: netting set definition
description: The definition of netting set is given in APS 180.

referenced_section_hint: Associations with Related Entities — related entity definition
description: Related-party exposure is defined by reference to the related entity definition in APS 222.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment D paragraph 41 — unrealised fair value losses on liquid assets
description: ADIs with liquid assets at amortised cost may adjust exposures for CET1 deductions to reflect unrealised fair value losses as allowed under APS 111 Attachment D paragraph 41.

referenced_section_hint: OTC and exchange-traded derivative transactions, long-settlement transactions, adjusted CEM
description: For significant financial institutions, EAD for OTC and exchange-traded derivative transactions or long-settlement transactions must be determined under APS 180.

referenced_section_hint: Liquidity — liquid assets
description: Liquid assets referenced in paragraph 19 are defined by reference to APS 210 Liquidity.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment E — defaulted property exposures
description: Defaulted property exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E rather than Attachment A.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment C — undrawn committed amounts for LVR calculation
description: Undrawn committed amounts included in the LVR loan amount calculation are determined in accordance with Attachment C.

referenced_section_hint: Independent property valuation requirements consistent with APS 220
description: Property valuations must be appraised independently using prudently conservative criteria consistent with the requirements set out in APS 220.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment D – unsettled and failed transactions
description: Unsettled and failed transactions must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment D to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment E – defaulted exposures
description: Defaulted exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment F – external credit rating requirements
description: Where Attachment B requires risk weights based on external credit ratings, ADIs must also comply with the requirements in Attachment F to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment A paragraphs 3 to 7 – standard loan criteria for covered bond cover pool assets
description: Residential and commercial property exposures in a covered bond cover pool must meet the standard loan requirements in paragraphs 3 to 7 of Attachment A for covered bonds issued in Australia.

referenced_section_hint: paragraphs 15 and 16 – covered bond requirements
description: Unrated or non-qualifying covered bonds must be treated as bank exposures per Table 7; qualifying criteria are set out in paragraphs 15 and 16 of Attachment B.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 41 – exposures through a third party
description: Exposures through a third party as detailed in paragraph 41 must not be included as corporate exposures.

referenced_section_hint: deduction of subordinated debt from regulatory capital
description: Subordinated debt not required to be deducted from regulatory capital under APS 111 must be risk-weighted at 150%.

referenced_section_hint: deduction of equity exposures from Regulatory Capital; deduction threshold in Attachment D to APS 111
description: Equity exposures not required to be deducted from Regulatory Capital under APS 111 must be risk-weighted at 250% or 400%; the risk-weighting of equity in banking or insurance subsidiaries at Level 1 is subject to the deduction threshold in Attachment D to APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: Attachments A, B and D – risk-weighting requirements
description: After applying CCFs, ADIs must risk-weight off-balance sheet exposures according to the requirements in Attachments A, B and D to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: senior securitisation exposures and resecuritisation exposures definitions
description: Attachment G references APS 120 for the definition of senior securitisation exposures eligible as financial collateral and for the exclusion of resecuritisation exposures from eligible collateral.

referenced_section_hint: qualifying central counterparties definition and adjusted CEM for OTC derivatives
description: Attachment G references APS 180 for the definition of qualifying central counterparties as core market participants and for the adjusted CEM methodology applicable to OTC derivatives under the comprehensive approach.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment B — risk weights for counterparties including banks and financial institutions
description: Attachment G cross-references Attachment B of APS 112 for determining risk weights applicable to third-party banks holding cash collateral and for identifying sovereigns and financial institutions qualifying as core market participants.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment J — criteria for credit derivatives and cash-funded credit-linked notes
description: Attachment G references Attachment J of APS 112 for the criteria that cash-funded credit-linked notes must fulfil to be treated as cash collateralised transactions.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment I — criteria for guarantees used for risk-weighting purposes
description: Attachment F references Attachment I of APS 112 for the criteria that must be satisfied before a guarantee from a rated entity can be recognised for risk-weighting purposes for other entities within the same corporate group.

referenced_section_hint: eligible bilateral netting agreement requirements for SFTs
description: Paragraph 33 requires that SFTs covered by an eligible bilateral netting agreement must meet the requirements detailed in Attachment H to this Prudential Standard.

referenced_section_hint: simple or comprehensive approach for RWA calculation and eligible collateral criteria
description: Paragraphs 6 and 8 of Attachment H reference Attachment G for eligible collateral criteria in the banking book and for the simple or comprehensive approach to RWA calculation.

referenced_section_hint: deduction of retained first loss position from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital
description: Paragraph 6 of Attachment I requires that a guarantee with a materiality threshold equivalent to a retained first loss position must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital in accordance with APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: definition of securitisation exposure
description: Footnote 24 in Table 23 states that securitisation exposure has the meaning given in APS 120.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 32 of Attachment G
description: The currency mismatch haircut on guarantees must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: synthetic securitisation requirements
description: Where an ADI buys credit protection through a credit derivative forming part of a synthetic securitisation, the requirements of APS 120 apply.

referenced_section_hint: Attachment G – cash-collateralised transactions
description: Cash-funded credit linked notes satisfying minimum credit derivative requirements may be treated as cash-collateralised transactions under Attachment G to APS 112.

referenced_section_hint: Common Equity Tier 1 Capital deductions
description: A credit derivative with a materiality threshold on payments must be deducted from Common Equity Tier 1 Capital in accordance with APS 111.

referenced_section_hint: synthetic securitisation requirements
description: Where partial credit derivative coverage involves a seniority difference between protected and unprotected portions, the arrangement is treated as a synthetic securitisation subject to APS 120.

referenced_section_hint: paragraph 32 of Attachment G
description: The currency mismatch haircut on credit derivatives must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.
